skill.name: neon-postgres
skill.description: Guides and best practices for working with Neon Serverless Postgres. Covers getting started, local development with Neon, choosing a connection method, Neon features, authentication (@neondatabase/auth), PostgREST-style data API (@neondatabase/neon-js), Neon CLI, and Neon's Platform API/SDKs. Use for any Neon-related questions.
skill.summary: Comprehensive guide for Neon Serverless Postgres covering setup, drivers, CLI tools, Admin API, authentication, branching, autoscaling, and connection management.
skill.author: neondatabase

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The detected patterns are Markdown documentation formatting (backticks for inline code), documentation URLs linking to official Neon resources, and file extension references (.md for Markdown). This is an informational skill providing guides and documentation links with no executable code or security risks.

content.value_statement: Building with serverless databases requires understanding connection methods, scaling behavior, and platform-specific features. This skill provides comprehensive guidance for working with Neon Serverless Postgres across all development scenarios.
actual_capabilities: Guide setup and configuration of Neon Serverless Postgres projects
Explain connection methods including TCP, HTTP, WebSocket, and serverless drivers
Provide instructions for Neon CLI, Admin API, and SDKs (TypeScript and Python)
Document branching, autoscaling, scale-to-zero, and instant restore features
Assist with authentication setup using Neon Auth integration
Explain connection pooling, read replicas, and IP allow list configuration
limitations: References external documentation - users must verify current API behavior
Does not execute commands or manage Neon resources directly
Requires network access to fetch official Neon documentation when needed
Platform-specific features may vary by Neon subscription plan

use_cases.title: Full-Stack Developer
use_cases.description: Set up Neon for a Next.js application with proper connection strings, serverless drivers, and authentication integration for production deployments.

use_cases.title: DevOps Engineer
use_cases.description: Automate database branching for preview environments, implement CI/CD pipelines with neonctl, and manage multiple projects via the Admin API.

use_cases.title: Data Engineer
use_cases.description: Configure read replicas for analytics workloads, set up logical replication for CDC pipelines, and optimize connection pooling for high-concurrency scenarios.

prompt_templates.title: Getting Started with Neon
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up a new Neon Serverless Postgres project. I need to create an organization, set up a project with branching enabled, get my connection string, and connect using a Node.js application with the appropriate driver.

prompt_templates.title: Choose the Right Connection Method
prompt_templates.prompt: I'm deploying to Vercel serverless functions with strict cold start requirements. Compare the available Neon connection methods and recommend the best approach for my use case, including connection pooling configuration.

prompt_templates.title: Implement Database Branching Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up a branching workflow for my team where each pull request gets an isolated database branch. Show me how to create branches programmatically using the Neon CLI and Admin API, and how to handle branch lifecycle management.

prompt_templates.title: Production Readiness Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Neon configuration for production deployment. Cover connection pooling settings, IP allow lists, scale-to-zero behavior, autoscaling limits, backup and restore strategy, and monitoring best practices.

output_examples.input: How do I connect to Neon from a Next.js app deployed on Vercel?
output_examples.output: For Next.js on Vercel, use the @neondatabase/serverless driver with HTTP-based connections. This avoids TCP socket issues in serverless runtimes. Configure your connection string with the -pooler endpoint for connection pooling, and set up the driver with proper pool configuration for your concurrency needs.

output_examples.input: What's the difference between branching and read replicas?
output_examples.output: Branches are copy-on-write clones for isolated development environments with their own compute endpoint - ideal for testing and preview deployments. Read replicas are read-only compute endpoints sharing the same storage - designed for scaling read-heavy workloads. Use branches for development isolation, read replicas for production scale.

best_practices: Always use connection pooling (-pooler endpoint) in serverless environments to handle bursty concurrency safely
Fetch current documentation from neon.com/docs before implementing - verify API behavior against official docs
Use branches for isolated testing and schema migrations, leveraging instant restore for point-in-time recovery
anti_patterns: Do not use direct TCP connections in serverless runtimes - use HTTP-based serverless driver instead
Avoid guessing documentation URLs - use the docs index at neon.com/docs/llms.txt to find current pages
Do not ignore cold-start latency when using scale-to-zero - budget hundreds of milliseconds for first query after suspend

faq.question: What is Neon Serverless Postgres?
faq.answer: Neon is a serverless PostgreSQL platform that separates compute and storage, providing autoscaling, instant branching, scale-to-zero, and instant restore. It is fully Postgres-compatible and works with any Postgres client.

faq.question: How does branching work in Neon?
faq.answer: Branches use copy-on-write semantics, creating instant isolated environments without duplicating data. Each branch gets its own compute endpoint, enabling safe schema testing and preview deployments.

faq.question: What driver should I use for serverless functions?
faq.answer: Use @neondatabase/serverless for serverless runtimes like Vercel or Cloudflare Workers. It uses HTTP/WebSocket connections instead of TCP, avoiding socket limitations in ephemeral environments.

faq.question: How does scale-to-zero affect performance?
faq.answer: Idle computes suspend after 5 minutes by default. The first query after suspend has a cold-start penalty of hundreds of milliseconds. Storage remains active during suspension.

faq.question: Do I need connection pooling?
faq.answer: Yes, in serverless or high-concurrency environments. Add -pooler to your endpoint hostname to use PgBouncer-based pooling, preventing connection exhaustion.

faq.question: Can I restore data from a previous state?
faq.answer: Yes, Neon supports instant restore to any point within your plan's retention window. You can also create branches from historical points for time-travel queries and recovery workflows.
